title: Python Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Python source files contain code written in the Python programming language, one of the most widely used general-purpose languages for scripting, data science, machine learning, web development, and automation.

## When to use
Use [Python Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:python;textable;code) for any `.py` source file, including application code, scripts, and test files. The `.pyw` extension indicates a Windows GUI script that suppresses the console window; `.pyi` files are stub files used for static type checking and should be treated as Python source.

## Notes
Python source is plain text encoded in UTF-8 (PEP 3120). The language lacks a compiled binary form distributed to end users; it runs via the CPython interpreter or alternatives such as PyPy. Implementers performing static analysis should account for version differences (Python 2 vs. Python 3) as the syntax is not fully compatible between major versions.
